originalColorSources.#37CA37: CSS variable --primary (strongest signal — this IS the brand green)
originalColorSources.#188BF6: CSS variable --secondary (default theme var, not used in the navbar/logo/CTAs — DROPPED)
originalColorSources.#2DFB5D: Sampled from scraped elements (neon variant of the same brand green — consolidated into accent family)

contrastIssues: Note: #37CA37 on pure white only hits 2.3:1 — fails large text. Kit uses dark mode to avoid this.
colorAudit.harmonyType: mono-accent (single electric green signal on near-black base — Nike/UFC sportswear system)
colorAudit.hierarchyStatus: strong — one electric accent, no competing colors, pure tonal contrast handles the rest
colorAudit.saturationNotes: Electric green is high-saturation by design and reads as a signal, not a brand flaw. Neutrals stay pure (no tint) to amplify the green.

colorShifts: Dropped secondary blue #188BF6 — it was only a default CSS variable, never used in the actual navbar/logo/CTAs
Pulled the site from light-on-white (GHL template default) to true dark (#0A0A0A) — matches Nike/UFC performance-athletic aesthetic the user requested
Kept #37CA37 EXACTLY as the brand accent — it's the signature electric green from their CSS and must be preserved
Added tonal steps (#141414 card, #1C1C1C elev, #262626 border) for dense, gallery-style composition without breaking the mono-accent system
Reserved #2DFB5D-family brightness for the accent-hover state (#4FE04F) instead of using it as a separate color — keeps the brand single-signal

designIntent.direction: Bold Nike × UFC sportswear gym system — hard edges, condensed display type, oversized numerals, kinetic motion, one electric signal color. Zero gradients, zero sci-fi clichés (no stars, no grids, no nebula).
designIntent.alienMotifHandling: Keep the 👽 as a tiny Easter-egg signature only — footer mark 'I BELIEVE 👽', one hidden repeat in the scrolling marquee, small glyph inside the 'BELIEVE' wordmark treatment. Never use it as hero artwork, never add space/galaxy imagery, never add purple/navy 'space' tones. The alien is the team's inside-joke mark — not a theme.
designIntent.typographicMood: UFC weigh-in board — huge condensed numerals, tight-tracked uppercase, unapologetic scale
designIntent.photographyMood: High-contrast b&w or hard-lit color of rolling, sweat, grips, belts — no stock smiling, no fake intensity

fontRecommendation.display: Barlow Condensed 800-900 uppercase (Nike sportswear condensed headlines, numerals, tags)
fontRecommendation.body: Inter 400-600 (clean neutral body, schedule grids, form fields)
fontRecommendation.mono: JetBrains Mono 500 (tiny data callouts — class times, belt ranks, file-header style)
fontRecommendation.reasoning: Barlow Condensed has the exact UFC/Nike weigh-in-card energy the user asked for — massive condensed uppercase headlines that feel like fight-night typography. Inter keeps the body copy neutral and readable so the accent carries all the emotional weight. JetBrains Mono for small data labels gives a performance-lab data feel without going full training-lab.

use: Kinetic marquee strips with 'BELIEVE · BELIEVE · 👽 · BELIEVE' — the alien shows up once per 3-4 repeats
Oversized numbers for class times, ages, belt tiers (weigh-in-card energy)
Diagonal accents, hard horizontal rules, tight uppercase tags
Single-color electric-green signal on black — every CTA, every active state
Tiny 👽 glyph inside the footer 'I BELIEVE' sign-off and inside one scrolling marquee
avoid: Purple or navy tones — breaks the single-signal system and triggers the spacey feel
Multiple alien characters, flying saucers as hero art, UFO decorations
Generic BJJ stock photos of white-gi silhouettes on white backgrounds
